ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_runs" (
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test run */,
  "testname" TEXT /* Name of the test executed */,
  "runs" INTEGER /* Total number of runs executed */,
  "wf_passed" INTEGER /* Number of workflows that passed */,
  "wf_failed" INTEGER /* Number of workflows that failed */,
  "passed" INTEGER /* Number of tests that passed */,
  "failed" INTEGER /* Number of tests that failed */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the test run started */,
  "num" INTEGER /* Identifier for the specific run */,
  "job" TEXT /* Job under which the test was executed */,
  "commit" TEXT /* Commit hash of the code being tested */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the test run belongs */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test run was executed */,
  "build_id" TEXT /* ID of the build associated with the test run */,
  "build_status" TEXT /* Status of the build */,
  "test_duration" TEXT /* Total duration of the test run */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment setup used for the test run */,
  "test_config" TEXT /* Configuration settings used for the test run */,
  "test_artifacts" TEXT /* Links or paths to test artifacts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("run_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_job FOREIGN KEY ("job") REFERENCES jobs ("job_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_commit FOREIGN KEY ("commit") REFERENCES commit_details ("commit_hash")
)

CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job */,
  "job_name" TEXT /* Name of the job */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the job belongs */,
  "scheduler" TEXT /* Scheduler used for the job */,
  "trigger_type" TEXT /* How the job is triggered */,
  "last_run_date" TEXT /* Date of the last job run */,
  "next_scheduled_date" TEXT /* Next scheduled date for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "commit_details" (
  "commit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each commit */,
  "commit_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the commit */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the commit */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the commit */,
  "branch" TEXT /* Branch of the commit */,
  "commit_message" TEXT /* Commit message */,
  "commit_url" TEXT /* URL to the commit in the version control system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("commit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_names" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the test */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the test does */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the test belongs */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of test (e.g., unit, integration, system) */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test is designed to run */,
  "test_timeout" INTEGER /* Timeout for the test */,
  "test_owners" TEXT /* Owners or maintainers of the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test run */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the test (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the test in seconds */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was executed */,
  "test_log" TEXT /* Log output of the test */,
  "test_artifacts" TEXT /* Links or paths to test artifacts */,
  "test_retries" INTEGER /* Number of retries for the test */,
  "test_flaky" TEXT /* Indicates if the test is known to be flaky */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_names ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_run_id FOREIGN KEY ("run_id") REFERENCES test_runs ("run_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "flake_tests" (
  "flake_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flake test record */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test */,
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test run */,
  "flake_count" INTEGER /* Count of how many times the test has been flaky */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the flaky test was executed */,
  "test_retries" INTEGER /* Number of retries for the flaky test */,
  "test_log" TEXT /* Log output of the flaky test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flake_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_flake_tests_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_names ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_flake_tests_run_id FOREIGN KEY ("run_id") REFERENCES test_runs ("run_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notification_settings" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who receives notifications */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for notifications */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., immediate, daily summary) */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the notification setting belongs */,
  "notification_level" TEXT /* Level of notifications (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "notification_channels" TEXT /* Channels for notifications (e.g., email, Slack, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notification_settings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, tester, admin) */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the user belongs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "last_password_change" TEXT /* Date and time of the last password change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the role belongs */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the access log belongs */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* HTTP status code of the access request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_flakes" (
  "flake_summary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flake summary */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test */,
  "total_flakes" INTEGER /* Total number of flake occurrences */,
  "last_flake_date" TEXT /* Date of the last flake occurrence */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the flake summary belongs */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the flaky test was executed */,
  "test_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of flaky test occurrences */,
  "test_log" TEXT /* Log output of the flaky test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flake_summary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_flakes_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_names ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workflows" (
  "workflow_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workflow */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the workflow */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the workflow */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project to which the workflow belongs */,
  "workflow_type" TEXT /* Type of workflow (e.g., build, test, deploy) */,
  "last_run_date" TEXT /* Date of the last workflow run */,
  "next_scheduled_date" TEXT /* Next scheduled date for the workflow */,
  "workflow_status" TEXT /* Status of the workflow (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workflow_id")
)
